failure_surface: Generated formulas used incorrect absolute/relative cell references, causing logical mismatch with the original formula intent.
root_cause: Agent replaced $B$2 (current week number reference) with $B$row_idx for each row, but rows 3-14 contained no valid week numbers. The original formula consistently referenced B$2 as the comparison value, which should remain fixed across all target cells.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: When generating row-wise formula variants, preserve the absolute reference pattern from the original formula for comparison values while only adjusting the data range references appropriately.
evidence: Original formula used $B$2 consistently as comparison: IF(D$2>=$B$2,D3,0)+IF(E$2>=$B$2,E3,0)+...
Agent's formula changed comparison reference per row: =SUMPRODUCT(--(D2:BC2>=$B$3), D3:BC3)
Row 2 contains week number (B2=31), but rows 3-14 have empty/None in column B
Formula logic requires constant comparison against current week in B2, not variable per-row values

memory_items.title: Preserve Reference Pattern Integrity Across Formula Variants
memory_items.lesson: When creating formula versions for multiple rows/columns, maintain the same absolute/relative reference pattern as the original formula for comparison anchors.
memory_items.generalization: Analyze which cell references should stay fixed vs. which should iterate before generating batch formulas. Lock comparison references that represent global values.

memory_items.title: Validate Data Presence Before Creating Comparison-Based Formulas
memory_items.lesson: Ensure comparison target cells contain valid data before writing formulas that depend on them.
memory_items.generalization: Check that reference cells have meaningful values. If comparison values exist only in one location (e.g., header row), ensure all formulas reference that location, not the iteration row.

memory_items.title: Cross-Reference Original Formula Logic During Optimization
memory_items.lesson: When optimizing complex formulas (replacing nested IFs with SUMPRODUCT/AVERAGEIFS, etc.), map each original component to the new formula's equivalent.
memory_items.generalization: For condition-based aggregation optimizations, verify that lookup/value references match the original intent. Test a single cell result against manual calculation.

edits.content:
**Formula Reference Preservation**: When generating formula variants across multiple cells, maintain consistent absolute/relative reference patterns from the original formula for comparison anchors. Identify which references should be locked (global comparison values) vs. which should vary (data row ranges).

**Data Validation for Comparison Cells**: Before writing conditional formulas, verify that comparison target cells contain valid data across all iterations. If comparison values reside in a single location (e.g., header row), ensure all generated formulas reference that fixed location.

**Optimization Verification**: When replacing complex formulas (nested IFs with SUMPRODUCT, COUNTIF, etc.), map each original component to the optimized equivalent and validate at least one formula result manually to confirm logical equivalence.
